<local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
<local-command-stdout>Set model to [1mOpus 4.8 (1M context) (default)[22m and saved as your default for new sessions</local-command-stdout>

Survey the CMK spec pair for emphasized words that should be quoin
  linked-term references instead. Survey only — make no edits.

  Scope:
  - Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/MCM-MetaConceptModel.adoc
  - Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/AXLA-Lexicon.adoc

  Meta-level caution, specific to these documents: MCM governs concept-model
  formatting itself, so its prose discusses asterisks, emphasis, attribute
  references, and anchors AS CONTENT. A span like *bold* may be an
  illustration of markup, not a term mention. Before classifying any hit,
  read enough context to decide whether the emphasis is (a) the document
  USING emphasis in its own prose voice — classifiable below — or (b) the
  document DEMONSTRATING markup syntax — always LEGITIMATE. When genuinely
  unsure, put it in the judgment-call list rather than guessing.

  Method: find every bold/italic emphasis span (single- and double-asterisk)
  containing a domain concept word. For each prose-voice hit, classify:

  1. DRIFT — a quoin for the concept already exists (mcm_ quoins in MCM,
     axl_/axk_ quoins in AXLA) but the prose uses emphasis instead of
     {attr_ref}. Report file, line, current text, and the quoin it should
     reference.
  2. MINT CANDIDATE — the emphasized word names a real domain concept with
     no quoin in either document. Report where it's used and how often; do
     not propose prefixes or anchors — minting is a separate operator
     decision.
  3. LEGITIMATE — leave alone: markup demonstrations per the caution above,
     run-in labels (*Invocation:*, *Design rationale:* and similar),
     anything inside ---- literal/listing blocks, genuine stress emphasis
     on ordinary words (*not*, *every*), and spans already carrying a quoin
     ref inside the emphasis.

  MCM and AXLA are siblings in one kit and reference each other by design
  (voicings, motifs), so a cross-document quoin reference is not
  automatically suspect — but verify the attribute actually resolves in the
  referencing document's mapping section; an emphasis standing in for a
  sibling's quoin is still DRIFT if the attribute is available, and a
  judgment call if it isn't.

  Deliverable: a per-file table of DRIFT findings, a frequency-ranked list
  of MINT candidates, and the judgment-call list. No edits, no commits.
  End with a rough sizing: is the DRIFT bucket sed/Haiku-grade mechanical,
  or does it need restructuring (MCM line-break discipline applies to any
  restructured line)?

## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll survey both documents. Let me start by reading them in full to understand their structure, especially the mapping sections where quoins are registered.

88	:mcm_annotation_s:           <<mcm_annotation,Annotations>>

90	:mcm_form_bullet:            <<mcm_form_bullet,Bullet Form>>
91	:mcm_form_section:           <<mcm_form_section,Section Form>>
92	
93	// end::mapping-section[]
94	
95	== Core Structure
96	
97	A
98	{mcm_concept_model}
99	creates a cohesive gestalt - a unified constellation of ideas whose relationships and interactions transcend their individual definitions.
100	Through carefully curated terminology and precise linking patterns, each
101	{mcm_concept_model}
102	illuminates the deep conceptual relationships that give meaning to a domain.
103	
104	The power of a
105	{mcm_concept_model}
106	lies in its ability to:
107	
108	* Reveal the natural connections between ideas
109	* Preserve the integrity of concept relationships through evolution
110	* Enable clear visualization of how changes to one concept ripple through the constellation
111	* Guide understanding through carefully mapped idea spaces
112	
113	Each
114	{mcm_concept_model}
115	manifests through a
116	{mcm_document}
117	that follows strict organizational patterns.
118	These patterns ensure that as the constellation evolves, their relationships remain clear and traceable.
119	
120	The
121	{mcm_document}
122	contains two primary constrained element types:
123	
124	* A
125	{mcm_mapping_section}
126	that defines all
127	{mcm_linked_term_s}
128	* One or more
129	{mcm_definition_section_s}
130	that provide precise meaning for each term
131	
132	Through these elements, the
133	{mcm_document}
134	maintains the delicate balance between rigorous structure and organic concept evolution, allowing the idea constellation to grow while preserving its essential relationships.
135	
136	== Text Organization
137	
138	Text within a
139	{mcm_document}
140	follows specific structural rules to enable clear tracking of changes and relationships.
141	
142	=== Line Break Fundamentals
143	
144	The most fundamental organizing principle is the
145	{mcm_line_break}
146	.
147	Each
148	{mcm_line_break}
149	creates a clear boundary in the
150	{mcm_document}
151	while preserving fluid rendering in the output document.
152	
153	==== Basic Line Break Rules
154	
155	{mcm_line_break_s}
156	occur in these precise patterns:
157	
158	* Between independent sentences
159	* Before and after each
160	{mcm_linked_term}
161	(including each term within a sequence)
162	* Between fragments that surround
163	{mcm_linked_term_s}
164	
165	Basic example showing sentence and term breaks:
166	
167	[source,asciidoc]
168	----
169	The system initializes its components.
170	The
171	{excm_processor}
172	begins operation.
173	Each component reports its status.
174	----
175	
176	==== Term Integration Patterns
177	
178	When incorporating
179	{mcm_linked_term_s}
180	the text follows these specific patterns:
181	
182	Single term integration - breaks before and after the term:
183	
184	[source,asciidoc]
185	----
186	The system processes
187	{excm_request}
188	according to defined rules.
189	----
190	
191	Multiple
192	{mcm_linked_term_s}
193	in sequence - each term gets its own line, with commas attached:
194	
195	[source,asciidoc]
196	----
197	The interface supports
198	{excm_drag},
199	{excm_drop},
200	and
201	{excm_scroll}
202	operations.
203	----
204	
205	Complex
206	{mcm_linked_term}
207	relationships - each standalone term gets breaks:
208	
209	[source,asciidoc]
210	----
211	The
212	{excm_validator}
213	applies each
214	{excm_rule}
215	to ensure
216	{excm_compliance}
217	with system requirements.
218	----
219	
220	These patterns apply consistently across all document contexts, including:
221	
222	* Regular paragraphs
223	* List items
224	* Definition entries
225	* Section introductions
226	
227	=== Paragraph Organization
228	
229	Paragraphs in the
230	{mcm_document}
231	follow these structural principles:
232	
233	* {mcm_blank_line_s}
234	appear only between true paragraphs
235	* No
236	{mcm_blank_line_s}
237	between sentences within a paragraph
238	* No
239	{mcm_blank_line_s}
240	around source code blocks or list items
241	* Each paragraph focuses on a single concept or relationship
242	
243	Example paragraph structure:
244	
245	[source,asciidoc]
246	----
247	The system initializes its core components.
248	Each component performs self-diagnostics.
249	The
250	{excm_validator}
251	confirms system health.
252	
253	The initialization process then moves to external systems.
254	The
255	{excm_connector}
256	establishes required links.
257	Each connection undergoes verification.
258	----
259	
260	=== Special Cases
261	
262	==== List Items
263	
264	The
265	{mcm_document}
266	presents list items
267	with proper term integration
268	and conceptual breaks:
269	
270	[source,asciidoc]
271	----
272	* The first item spans
273	{excm_component}
274	boundaries
275	* A second item demonstrates
276	{excm_process}
277	flow
278	* The final item shows
279	{excm_result}
280	formatting
281	----
282	
283	==== Definition Entries
284	
285	Definition entries follow standard sentence breaks while maintaining definition list structure:
286	
287	The single space following `::` is necessary.
288	
289	[source,asciidoc]
290	----
291	[[excm_term]]
292	{excm_example}:: 
293	The primary definition establishes core meaning.
294	Additional sentences expand understanding.
295	The
296	{excm_linked_term}
297	shows relationship handling.
298	----
299	
300	==== Source Code Boundaries
301	
302	Source code blocks establish strict boundaries for
303	{mcm_curation_action_s}.
304	These boundaries protect code content while maintaining proper term integration in surrounding text:
305	
306	[source,asciidoc]
307	----
308	The
309	{excm_processor}
310	implements this interface:
311	
312	[source,java]
313	-----
314	public interface Processor {
315	    void process();
316	    Status check();
317	}
318	-----
319	
320	The implementation ensures
321	{excm_compliance}
322	with system requirements.
323	----
324	
325	{mcm_curation_action_s}
326	respect these boundaries by:
327	
328	* Maintaining term integration and line breaks in surrounding text
329	* Treating code block contents as opaque
330	* Preserving code formatting without modification
331	* Excluding code content from term matching and promotion analysis
332	
333	This boundary pattern ensures code examples remain stable through document evolution while maintaining proper conceptual integration in their context.
334	
335	=== Punctuation Rules
336	
337	Punctuation follows standard English conventions regardless of line breaks:
338	
339	* Sentence-ending punctuation stays with the sentence
340	* Commas remain with their preceding phrase
341	* Quotation marks enclose their content without breaks
342	* Parentheses maintain content unity
343	
344	Example punctuation handling:
345	
346	[source,asciidoc]
347	----
348	The system (version 2.0) activates its components.
349	The
350	{excm_processor}
351	begins its "primary sequence."
352	After initialization,
353	the system reports "ready."
354	----
355	
356	=== Document Mechanics
357	
358	=== {mcm_mapping_section} Constraints
359	
360	The
361	{mcm_mapping_section}
362	enforces alignment rules to enhance readability and change tracking:
363	
364	* The `<<` of each
365	{mcm_domain}
366	aligns to columns that are multiples of 10
367	* When entries require wider alignment, all lines adjust to the next larger multiple
368	* {mcm_attribute_reference_s}
369	maintain consistent indentation
370	
371	[source,asciidoc]
372	----
373	// tag::mapping-example[]
374	:excm_doc_short:              <<excm_short,Short Term>>
375	:excm_doc_longer_term:        <<excm_longer,Longer Term>>
376	:excm_doc_much_longer_term:   <<excm_much_longer,Much Longer Term>>
377	// end::mapping-example[]
378	----
379	
380	{mcm_normalization}
381	maintains these alignments during curation unless expansion is required.
382	
383	=== Document Source Management
384	
385	The
386	{mcm_document}
387	maintains compatibility with line-based tools
388	while preserving semantic structure.
389	
390	This means:
391	
392	* Favoring AsciiDoc's native list markers
393	over hardcoded sequence numbers
394	
395	* Preserving processor directives
396	that enable clean rendering
397	
398	* Ensuring changes remain visible
399	in both source differencing tools
400	and rendered comparisons
401	
402	The goal is maintaining a document that:
403	
404	* Shows ancestry clearly in source
405	* Renders cleanly through AsciiDoc
406	* Displays changes meaningfully in diffs
407	* Supports post-rendering analysis
408	
409	=== Term Hierarchy Management
410	
411	When
412	{mcm_linked_term_s}
413	appear to form hierarchical relationships, the
414	{mcm_document}
415	flattens this hierarchy through specific patterns:
416	
417	* Each
418	{mcm_linked_term_s}
419	receives first-class presentation in the
420	{mcm_mapping_section}
421	* Related
422	{mcm_linked_term_s}
423	maintain separate definitions
424	* Relationships express through careful definition crafting
425	
426	Example of hierarchical
427	{mcm_linked_term_s}
428	handling:
429	
430	[source,asciidoc]
431	----
432	:excm_doc_validation:             <<excm_validation,Validation>>
433	:excm_doc_validation_rule:        <<excm_validation_rule,Validation Rule>>
434	:excm_doc_validation_set:         <<excm_validation_set,Validation Set>>
435	----
436	
437	The definitions then express relationships:
438	
439	[source,asciidoc]
440	----
441	[[excm_validation]]
442	{excm_doc_validation}:: 
443	The process of applying rules to ensure content correctness.
444	
445	[[excm_validation_rule]]
446	{excm_doc_validation_rule}:: 
447	A specific constraint within the {excm_doc_validation} framework.
448	
449	[[excm_validation_set]]
450	{excm_doc_validation_set}:: 
451	A collection of {excm_doc_validation_rule_s} that work together.
452	----
453	
454	== Definition Patterns
455	
456	=== Definition Form Patterns
457	
458	Each
459	{mcm_linked_term}
460	in a
461	{mcm_document}
462	requires an anchor target and a definition.
463	The form determines how these elements are structured.
464	
465	[[mcm_annotation]]
466	{mcm_annotation}::
467	A comment line providing type categorization or metadata about terms in a
468	{mcm_document}.
469	{mcm_annotation_s}
470	are ignored during first-order document reading.
471	When a referencing system is in context,
472	{mcm_annotation_s}
473	provide structured metadata interpreted by that system.
474	The content contains space-separated
475	{mcm_linked_term_s}
476	from the referencing system, which is typically out-of-context during first-order reading.
477	The grammar, interpretation, and placement rules for
478	{mcm_annotation_s}
479	are defined by the referencing system, not MCM.
480	+
481	{mcm_annotation_s}
482	use prefix-discriminated form: `//ax...` — no space after `//`, where the initial characters match a registered prefix identifying the referencing system.
483	The full content after `//` is interpreted by the referencing system as space-separated prefix terms.
484	+
485	The mechanical discriminator is the character immediately following `//`.
486	A space character indicates a regular comment.
487	A letter with no intervening space indicates an
488	{mcm_annotation}.
489	+
490	Registered prefixes:
491	+
492	* `ax` — Axial Lexicon
493	
494	[[mcm_form_deflist]]
495	{mcm_form_deflist}::
496	The standard pattern using AsciiDoc definition list syntax.
497	The anchor target appears on its own line, followed by an optional
498	{mcm_annotation}
499	then the term reference with `::` and definition text.
500	Use for standalone terms with self-contained meaning.
501	
502	[source,asciidoc]
503	----
504	[[term_anchor]]
505	//axl_voices axk_motif
506	{term_reference}::
507	Definition text following sentence-per-line rules.
508	----
509	
510	[[mcm_form_bullet]]
511	{mcm_form_bullet}::
512	A pattern for terms that belong to a parent structure or sequence.
513	Each term appears as a bullet item under its parent section.
514	The anchor target appears after the bullet marker, followed by an optional
515	{mcm_annotation}
516	then the term reference with definition prose.
517	Use for fields of a structure or steps in a procedure.
518	
519	[source,asciidoc]
520	----
521	* [[field_anchor]]
522	//axl_voices axr_member
523	{field_reference} defines the specific meaning within the parent structure.
524	----
525	
526	[[mcm_form_section]]
527	{mcm_form_section}::
528	A pattern where the section heading itself is the term.
529	The anchor target appears before the section marker, followed by an optional
530	{mcm_annotation}
531	then the section heading containing the term reference.
532	The section content constitutes the definition.
533	Use for terms whose meaning requires extended explanation or contains nested child terms.
534	
535	[source,asciidoc]
536	----
537	[[sequence_anchor]]
538	//axl_voices axo_sequence
539	=== {sequence_reference}
540	
541	The sequence executes these steps:
542	
543	* [[step_one_anchor]]
544	//axl_voices axo_step
545	First step description.
546	----
547	
548	=== Definition Organization Patterns
549	
550	The
551	{mcm_document}
552	supports three primary patterns for organizing
553	{mcm_definition_section_s}:
554	
555	==== Traditional Grouped Definitions
556	
557	[source,asciidoc]
558	----
559	== Core Terms
560	
561	[[excm_term1]]
562	{excm_term_one}:: 
563	Primary definition establishing core meaning.
564	Subsequent sentences expand understanding, though this is at most one paragraph.
565	
566	[[excm_term2]]
567	{excm_term_two}:: 
568	Related term definition showing relationships.
569	----
570	
571	==== Distributed Definitions
572	
573	Definitions may accompany relevant content sections:
574	
575	[source,asciidoc]
576	----
577	== System Components
578	
579	Here we explore key architectural elements.
580	
581	[[excm_component]]
582	{excm_sys_component}:: 
583	Definition tied directly to its context.
584	
585	The discussion continues...
586	----
587	
588	==== Domain-Specific Glossaries
589	
590	[source,asciidoc]
591	----
592	== Technical Terms
593	
594	[[excm_tech1]]
595	{excm_tech_term_one}:: 
596	Technical definition with domain focus.
597	
598	[[excm_tech2]]
599	{excm_tech_term_two}:: 
600	Related technical concept definition.
601	----
602	
603	=== {mcm_category}, {mcm_variant}, and {mcm_domain} Usage
604	
605	The
606	{mcm_category},
607	{mcm_variant},
608	and
609	{mcm_domain}
610	work together to maintain precise term relationships and variations.
611	
612	==== {mcm_category} Rules
613	
614	* Each
615	{mcm_category}
616	declares its scope in the
617	{mcm_mapping_section}
618	header
619	* {mcm_category_ies}
620	distinguish terms by conceptual role
621	* New
622	{mcm_category_ies}
623	require explicit declaration
624	
625	Example
626	{mcm_category}
627	declarations:
628	
629	[source,asciidoc]
630	----
631	// exn_: Noun concepts - entities and objects
632	// exv_: Verb concepts - actions and operations
633	----
634	
635	==== Variant Patterns
636	
637	Common
638	{mcm_variant_s}
639	follow consistent patterns:
640	
641	[source,asciidoc]
642	----
643	:exn_word:                   <<excm_word,Word>>
644	:exn_word_s:                 <<excm_word,Words>>
645	:exn_word_p:                 <<excm_word,Word's>>
646	:exv_act:                    <<excm_act,Act>>
647	:exv_act_s:                  <<excm_act,Acts>>
648	:exv_act_ed:                 <<excm_act,Acted>>
649	:exv_act_ing:                <<excm_act,Acting>>
650	----
651	
652	{mcm_normalization}
653	enforces consistent application of these patterns across the document.
654	
655	==== {mcm_domain} Pattern
656	
657	Each term definition establishes a unique anchor using snake case:
658	
659	[source,asciidoc]
660	----
661	// Domain declaration: excm_<canonical_term>
662	----
663	
664	== Task Lens Support
665	
666	{mcm_concept_model_s}
667	support focused work contexts through
668	{mcm_task_lens_es}
669	that emphasize relevant concepts while suppressing distractions.
670	See the
671	{mcm_task_lens_guide}
672	for compilation specifications and usage patterns.
673	
674	=== Task Lens Structure
675	
676	Each
677	{mcm_task_lens}
678	follows this pattern:
679	
680	[source,asciidoc]
681	----
682	=== [Task Lens Name]
683	
684	**Purpose**: [One sentence describing the work this lens enables]
685	
686	**Gestalt**: [Metaphorical description guiding term interpretation]
687	
688	**Core Activation**:
689	
690	* {linked_term} - [Aspect emphasis if needed]
691	* {linked_term} - [Aspect emphasis if needed]
692	
693	**Expanded Activation**:
694	
695	* {linked_term} - [Reduced detail focus]
696	* {linked_term} - [Reduced detail focus]
697	
698	**Suppressed Activation**:
699	
700	* List of {linked_term_s} to remain dormant
701	* Additional suppression patterns
702	
703	**Critical Relationships**:
704	
705	* {term_a} ↔ {term_b}: [What aspect matters]
706	* {term_c} → {term_d}: [Specific mechanism focus]
707	----
708	
709	Multiple
710	{mcm_task_lens_es}
711	may share terms but apply different
712	{mcm_focal_depth_s}
713	and aspect emphasis based on their purpose.
714	
715	=== Blank Line Exception for Task Lenses
716	
717	Despite the general document rule against
718	{mcm_blank_line_s}
719	around list items,
720	{mcm_task_lens}
721	sections require specific blank lines for valid asciidoc parsing:
722	
723	* One blank line after each `**Section**:` header before its list
724	* One blank line between major sections
725	* No blank lines between list items within a section
726	
727	This exception exists because asciidoc requires blank lines before bulleted lists to parse correctly, and the structured nature of
728	{mcm_task_lens_es}
729	makes the blank line placement predictable and consistent.
730	
731	== Actor Definitions
732	
733	[[mcm_editor]]
734	{mcm_editor}::
735	The human originator of
736	{mcm_concept_model_s}
737	and constraints.
738	The
739	{mcm_editor}
740	guides document evolution, establishes patterns, and directs transformations while maintaining conceptual integrity.
741	
742	[[mcm_digital_mind]]
743	{mcm_digital_mind}::
744	An agent, typically an LLM, transforming
745	{mcm_document_s}
746	under
747	{mcm_editor}
748	direction and document constraints.
749	The
750	{mcm_digital_mind}
751	performs well-defined
752	{mcm_curation_action_s}
753	while preserving document patterns and relationships.
754	
755	== {mcm_curation_action_s}
756	
757	[[mcm_curation_action]]
758	{mcm_curation_action}::
759	A process executed by a
760	{mcm_digital_mind}
761	to adjust a
762	{mcm_document}
763	as directed by the
764	{mcm_editor}.
765	Each
766	{mcm_curation_action}
767	has a specific focus and constraints, modifying only those aspects of the
768	{mcm_document}
769	that fall within its defined scope.
770	
771	[[mcm_normalization]]
772	{mcm_normalization}::
773	A
774	{mcm_curation_action}
775	that brings a
776	{mcm_document}
777	to canonical form through two phases.
778	
779	*Phase 1: Text Normalization*
780	
781	Applies consistent formatting throughout the
782	{mcm_document}
783	body:
784	
785	* One sentence per line
786	* Each
787	{mcm_linked_term}
788	on its own line
789	* Commas and punctuation attached to preceding element
790	* {mcm_blank_line_s}
791	only between paragraphs and definition entries
792	* Preserve source code block contents without modification
793	
794	*Phase 2: Mapping Section Normalization*
795	
796	Maintains strict structural integrity of the
797	{mcm_mapping_section}:
798	
799	* Preserve
800	{mcm_category}
801	comment headers as
802	{mcm_category_group}
803	delimiters
804	* Within each
805	{mcm_category_group},
806	align `<<` to the smallest multiple of 10 columns that accommodates the longest
807	{mcm_attribute_reference}
808	* Maintain alphabetical ordering by display text within each
809	{mcm_category_group}
810	* One
811	{mcm_attribute_reference}
812	per line
813	
814	[[mcm_category_group]]
815	{mcm_category_group}::
816	A contiguous block of
817	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
818	in the
819	{mcm_mapping_section}
820	preceded by a category comment header.
821	Each
822	{mcm_category_group}
823	maintains independent column alignment,
824	allowing terms to be added without affecting other groups.
825	
826	[[mcm_demotion_analysis]]
827	{mcm_demotion_analysis}::
828	A
829	{mcm_curation_action}
830	that identifies
831	{mcm_linked_term_s}
832	whose formal status may no longer justify their maintenance overhead.
833	It suggests candidates for demotion to
834	{mcm_fundamental_text}
835	based on criteria such as:
836	* Rare usage outside their own definition
837	* Lack of meaningful variation requiring
838	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
839	* Minimal role in expressing relationships between other terms
840	* Terms whose precise meaning has become self-evident in context
841	
842	[[mcm_link_validation]]
843	{mcm_link_validation}::
844	A
845	{mcm_curation_action}
846	that verifies the mechanical integrity of the
847	{mcm_document_p}
848	linking system without modification:
849	
850	* Reports
851	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
852	that lack corresponding anchors
853	* Reports anchors that lack corresponding
854	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
855	* Validates
856	{mcm_domain}
857	format compliance
858	* Identifies malformed link syntax
859	
860	This action produces a diagnostic report for
861	{mcm_editor}
862	review.
863	Repairs are performed manually or through targeted
864	{mcm_curation_action_s}.
865	
866	[[mcm_promotion_analysis]]
867	{mcm_promotion_analysis}:: 
868	A
869	{mcm_curation_action}
870	that identifies frequently occurring or conceptually significant elements in
871	{mcm_fundamental_text}
872	that warrant promotion to
873	{mcm_linked_term}
874	status.
875	It suggests candidates based on:
876	* Frequency in definitions of other
877	{mcm_linked_term_s}
878	* Use in expressing core conceptual relationships
879	* Occurrence in multiple distinct contexts
880	* Words whose precise meaning needs maintenance across document evolution
881	
882	[[mcm_term_matching]]
883	{mcm_term_matching}::
884	A
885	{mcm_curation_action}
886	that identifies instances where concepts corresponding to existing
887	{mcm_linked_term_s}
888	appear in text without proper linking:
889	* Identifies important terms that warrant linking
890	* Proposes what should be linked
891	
892	[[mcm_validation_summary]]
893	{mcm_validation_summary}::
894	An advisory report produced by
895	{mcm_link_validation}
896	that documents the state of the
897	{mcm_document}
898	without making modifications.
899	The
900	{mcm_validation_summary}
901	enables the
902	{mcm_editor}
903	to identify issues requiring attention.
904	
905	== Word Selection
906	
907	Prefix discipline governs the left half of every persistent name;
908	this section governs the right half — the word itself.
909	The rules below bind the word part of every
910	{mcm_quoin},
911	the display text of every
912	{mcm_linked_term},
913	and any persistent name a system mints: verbs, nouns, file words, command words.
914	They constrain births, not the living —
915	existing names are swept only by deliberate remint tooling, never piecemeal.
916	
917	* *Semantic uniqueness.*
918	One meaning per word across all persistent names, repo-wide.
919	A word already bound to a meaning anywhere in the system is unavailable for a second meaning, however distant the domain.
920	* *Vocabulary isolation.*
921	Words mint from source domains alien to software, cloud infrastructure, CI/CD, and corporate project management.
922	A word with heavy ambient presence in software prose is a
923	{mcm_trodden_word}
924	and is disqualified regardless of semantic fit.
925	Domain choice makes collision structurally impossible;
926	word-level checks merely verify.
927	* *The grep gate.*
928	A candidate word is verified by repo-wide grep before adoption;
929	a clean landing is a minting precondition, not a nicety.
930	* *Rare but real.*
931	Prefer rare, real words whose true meaning does semantic work.
932	Concrete, physical, sensory images lodge across sessions in ways abstract labels cannot.
933	Abbreviations spend recognition to save characters — the wrong trade.
934	* *Mint into an* {mcm_asterism}.
935	A new word joins a coherent metaphor family wherever one fits;
936	a lone orphan word is weaker than a family member.
937	* *Audible register.*
938	Sibling subsystems ride
939	{mcm_asterism_s}
940	whose registers are distinguishable by ear,
941	so a stray term's home system is audible without lookup.
942	
943	[[mcm_asterism]]
944	{mcm_asterism}::
945	A named metaphor family whose source-domain relationships mirror the system relationships it names.
946	The name is the gestalt: a recognizable figure of stars that is not itself a constellation and may span several —
947	as a vocabulary family may span
948	{mcm_concept_model_s}.
949	Each term inherits relational meaning from the family
950	(the coronet sits below the firemark as the hoof marking sits below the brand),
951	so the metaphor performs structural work beyond retrieval,
952	and a new term in an established family arrives nearly pre-learned —
953	for the
954	{mcm_digital_mind}
955	and the
956	{mcm_editor}
957	alike.
958	Founding specimen: Job Jockey's equestrian vocabulary (JJS0, Equestrian Vocabulary), with the racing and dressage layers as sibling families sharing one register.
959	Model-side rationale: `Memos/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`;
960	prefix-side sibling: `Memos/memo-20260110-acronym-selection-study.md`.
961	
962	== Antipatterns
963	
964	[[mcm_antipattern]]
965	{mcm_antipattern}::
966	A named, recurring document defect formally catalogued so that
967	{mcm_curation_action_s}
968	and reviews can cite it precisely.
969	Naming the defect is what makes it detectable and dischargeable;
970	an unnamed defect recurs because no rule can forbid it.
971	
972	[[mcm_tidemark]]
973	{mcm_tidemark}::
974	An
975	{mcm_antipattern}:
976	version commentary embedded in a durable document — revision markers, dated change narration, "new in vX" annotations.
977	The name is the gestalt: the stain showing where the water once stood, saying nothing about where it is.
978	History-time knowledge belongs to the commit;
979	git log and blame carry every line's date and reason with a forcing function, while in-document
980	{mcm_tidemark_s}
981	drift and pollute present-tense reading.
982	A durable document — spec, guide, code, context file — speaks of itself only in the present tense.
983	A dated artifact — memo, scar, commit message — is itself a historical record and may carry revision metadata without fault.
984	Detection and drain moves are catalogued in ACG (ACGm_107).
985	
986	[[mcm_trodden_word]]
987	{mcm_trodden_word}::
988	An
989	{mcm_antipattern}:
990	a persistent name minted from vocabulary worn featureless by ambient software prose —
991	open, close, run, lock, task, manager, handler, grant.
992	The name is the gestalt: a path so heavily walked that no single footprint is findable on it;
993	grep returns a thousand strangers.
994	A
995	{mcm_trodden_word}
996	fails retrieval for the
997	{mcm_digital_mind}
998	and search for the
999	{mcm_editor},
1000	and invites the plan-versus-product bleed that vocabulary isolation exists to prevent.
1001	Semantic fit never rescues it:
1002	the word is disqualified before fit is weighed.
1003	
1004	== Document Elements
1005	
1006	[[mcm_fundamental_text]]
1007	{mcm_fundamental_text}:: 
1008	The basic sentences and phrases that comprise document text.
1009	{mcm_fundamental_text}
1010	follows specific formatting patterns in source while maintaining natural flow in rendered output.
1011	
1012	[[mcm_notional_snippet]]
1013	{mcm_notional_snippet}:: 
1014	An exemplar that guides
1015	{mcm_curation_action_s}.
1016	While it may contain asciidoc noncompliance, the
1017	{mcm_digital_mind}
1018	uses it to understand patterns and generate compliant asciidoc output.
1019	
1020	[[mcm_line_break]]
1021	{mcm_line_break}::
1022	A deliberate separation in a
1023	{mcm_document}
1024	that creates clear boundaries while preserving fluid rendering.
1025	The
1026	{mcm_line_break}
1027	appears between independent sentences, before and after
1028	{mcm_linked_term_s},
1029	and between fragments surrounding
1030	{mcm_linked_term_s}.
1031	This pattern enables clean ancestry tracking while maintaining natural document flow.
1032	
1033	[[mcm_blank_line]]
1034	{mcm_blank_line}::
1035	A purposeful demarcation in the
1036	{mcm_document}
1037	that separates major structural elements such as definition entries, sections, and lists.
1038	The
1039	{mcm_blank_line}
1040	appears only between these elements, never within them.
1041	
1042	== Term Management Elements
1043	
1044	[[mcm_attribute_reference]]
1045	{mcm_attribute_reference}::
1046	A variable-style reference that connects a
1047	{mcm_category}
1048	prefixed attribute name to a specific
1049	{mcm_domain}
1050	and replacement text potentially with multiple
1051	{mcm_variant_s}.
1052	The
1053	{mcm_attribute_reference}
1054	forms the mechanical foundation for
1055	{mcm_linked_term}
1056	implementation in the
1057	{mcm_document}.
1058	Each
1059	{mcm_attribute_reference}
1060	follows strict formatting rules defined in the
1061	{mcm_mapping_section}.
1062	
1063	[[mcm_quoin]]
1064	{mcm_quoin}::
1065	A formal identifier with full cataloguing in the
1066	{mcm_concept_model}:
1067	{mcm_attribute_reference}
1068	in the
1069	{mcm_mapping_section},
1070	anchor at the definition site, and definition text.
1071	The cornerstone that locks the structure — addressable from anywhere, tracked across the constellation.
1072	A
1073	{mcm_quoin}
1074	is the Diptych-era term for what
1075	{mcm_linked_term}
1076	describes in MCM's AsciiDoc expression.
1077	
1078	[[mcm_inlay]]
1079	{mcm_inlay}::
1080	A formal identifier following the prefix naming discipline that exists only within a subdocument's scope.
1081	No
1082	{mcm_attribute_reference},
1083	no anchor, no
1084	{mcm_mapping_section}
1085	entry.
1086	An
1087	{mcm_inlay}
1088	is set permanently into a surface — recognized by its prefix pattern — but not catalogued in the parent document's lexicon.
1089	Used by hierarchy markers that need to formally identify elements without elevating them to
1090	{mcm_quoin}
1091	status.
1092	
1093	[[mcm_sprue]]
1094	{mcm_sprue}::
1095	A literal wire-level token in backtick delimiters representing a serialized name.
1096	No
1097	{mcm_attribute_reference},
1098	no anchor, no
1099	{mcm_mapping_section}
1100	entry, no prefix naming discipline required.
1101	A
1102	{mcm_sprue}
1103	is the exact character sequence as it appears in a wire format — a JSON property key, an API field name, a protocol identifier.
1104	May originate from external schemas or foreign conventions.
1105	A
1106	{mcm_sprue}
1107	that follows the prefix naming discipline is also an
1108	{mcm_inlay};
1109	one that does not is recognized solely by its backtick delimiters.
1110	Used by arity rules when annotations read literal wire names from following text.
1111	
1112	[[mcm_category]]
1113	{mcm_category}::
1114	A prefix mechanism that classifies
1115	{mcm_linked_term_s}
1116	by their conceptual role.
1117	Each
1118	{mcm_category}
1119	must be declared in the
1120	{mcm_mapping_section}
1121	header and follows consistent patterns for classifying terms by type (e.g., 'doc_' for document elements, 'term_' for terminology elements).
1122	
1123	[[mcm_variant]]
1124	{mcm_variant}::
1125	A systematic modification of a base term that maintains its core meaning while expressing a different form.
1126	Common
1127	{mcm_variant_s}
1128	include plurals (_s), possessives (_p), and verb conjugations (_ed, _ing), each following patterns defined in the
1129	{mcm_mapping_section}.
1130	
1131	[[mcm_domain]]
1132	{mcm_domain}::
1133	A unique identifier that anchors a term's definition within the document structure.
1134	Each
1135	{mcm_domain}
1136	uses snake case formatting and maintains consistent patterns (e.g., 'mcm_<canonical_term>') to enable reliable linking and reference.
1137	
1138	[[mcm_task_lens]]
1139	{mcm_task_lens}::
1140	A focused activation pattern within a
1141	{mcm_concept_model}
1142	that emphasizes specific terms and relationships for particular work contexts.
1143	Each
1144	{mcm_task_lens}
1145	defines which
1146	{mcm_linked_term_s}
1147	activate at what
1148	{mcm_focal_depth}
1149	and guides interpretation through gestalt descriptions and critical relationship specifications.
1150	
1151	
1152	[[mcm_task_lens_guide]]
1153	{mcm_task_lens_guide}::
1154	A separate guide document that specifies how to compile task-focused representations from a full
1155	{mcm_concept_model}.
1156	The
1157	{mcm_task_lens_guide}
1158	defines lens structure patterns, compilation mechanics, and
1159	{mcm_focal_depth}
1160	activation rules for creating work-optimized views of concept constellations.
1161	
1162	[[mcm_lens_specification]]
1163	{mcm_lens_specification}::
1164	The structured definition within a
1165	{mcm_task_lens}
1166	that categorizes
1167	{mcm_linked_term_s}
1168	by their
1169	{mcm_focal_depth}
1170	and provides aspect focusing guidance.
1171	The
1172	{mcm_lens_specification}
1173	enables mechanical compilation of task-optimized representations from the full
1174	{mcm_concept_model}.
1175	
1176	[[mcm_focal_depth]]
1177	{mcm_focal_depth}::
1178	The activation level of a
1179	{mcm_linked_term}
1180	within a
1181	{mcm_task_lens},
1182	determining how much detail and relationship information to preserve.
1183	The three
1184	{mcm_focal_depth_s}
1185	are Core (full detail with relationships), Expanded (supporting context), and Suppressed (explicitly dormant).
1186

file.content:
= Concept Model Definition
:doctype: book

// tag::mapping-section[]
//
// Category declarations:
// mcm_: Meta Concept Model
//
// Variant patterns:
//   _s: plural form
//   _p: possessive form
//   _ed: past tense (verbs)
//   _ing: progressive form (verbs)
//
// Domain declaration: mcm_<canonical_term>

:mcm_digital_mind:           <<mcm_digital_mind,Digital Mind>>
:mcm_digital_mind_s:         <<mcm_digital_mind,Digital Minds>>
:mcm_editor:                 <<mcm_editor,Editor>>
:mcm_editor_p:               <<mcm_editor,Editor's>>

:mcm_concept_model:          <<mcm_concept_model,Concept Model>>
:mcm_concept_model_s:        <<mcm_concept_model,Concept Models>>
:mcm_concept_model_p:        <<mcm_concept_model,Concept Model's>>

:mcm_category_group:         <<mcm_category_group,Category Group>>
:mcm_category_group_s:       <<mcm_category_group,Category Groups>>
:mcm_curation_action:        <<mcm_curation_action,Curation Action>>
:mcm_curation_action_s:      <<mcm_curation_action,Curation Actions>>
:mcm_demotion_analysis:      <<mcm_demotion_analysis,Demotion Analysis>>
:mcm_link_validation:        <<mcm_link_validation,Link Validation>>
:mcm_normalization:          <<mcm_normalization,Normalization>>
:mcm_promotion_analysis:     <<mcm_promotion_analysis,Promotion Analysis>>
:mcm_term_matching:          <<mcm_term_matching,Term Matching>>
:mcm_validation_summary:     <<mcm_validation_summary,Validation Summary>>

:mcm_antipattern:            <<mcm_antipattern,Antipattern>>
:mcm_antipattern_s:          <<mcm_antipattern,Antipatterns>>

== Core Structure

A
{mcm_concept_model}
creates a cohesive gestalt - a unified constellation of ideas whose relationships and interactions transcend their individual definitions.
Through carefully curated terminology and precise linking patterns, each
{mcm_concept_model}
illuminates the deep conceptual relationships that give meaning to a domain.

The power of a
{mcm_concept_model}
lies in its ability to:

* Reveal the natural connections between ideas
* Preserve the integrity of concept relationships through evolution
* Enable clear visualization of how changes to one concept ripple through the constellation
* Guide understanding through carefully mapped idea spaces

Each
{mcm_concept_model}
manifests through a
{mcm_document}
that follows strict organizational patterns.
These patterns ensure that as the constellation evolves, their relationships remain clear and traceable.

The
{mcm_document}
contains two primary constrained element types:

* A
{mcm_mapping_section}
that defines all
{mcm_linked_term_s}
* One or more
{mcm_definition_section_s}
that provide precise meaning for each term

Through these elements, the
{mcm_document}
maintains the delicate balance between rigorous structure and organic concept evolution, allowing the idea constellation to grow while preserving its essential relationships.

== Text Organization

Text within a
{mcm_document}
follows specific structural rules to enable clear tracking of changes and relationships.

=== Line Break Fundamentals

The most fundamental organizing principle is the
{mcm_line_break}
.
Each
{mcm_line_break}
creates a clear boundary in the
{mcm_document}
while preserving fluid rendering in the output document.

==== Basic Line Break Rules

{mcm_line_break_s}
occur in these precise patterns:

* Between independent sentences
* Before and after each
{mcm_linked_term}
(including each term within a sequence)
* Between fragments that surround
{mcm_linked_term_s}

Basic example showing sentence and term breaks:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The system initializes its components.
The
{excm_processor}
begins operation.
Each component reports its status.
----

==== Term Integration Patterns

When incorporating
{mcm_linked_term_s}
the text follows these specific patterns:

Single term integration - breaks before and after the term:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The system processes
{excm_request}
according to defined rules.
----

Multiple
{mcm_linked_term_s}
in sequence - each term gets its own line, with commas attached:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The interface supports
{excm_drag},
{excm_drop},
and
{excm_scroll}
operations.
----

Complex
{mcm_linked_term}
relationships - each standalone term gets breaks:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The
{excm_validator}
applies each
{excm_rule}
to ensure
{excm_compliance}
with system requirements.
----

These patterns apply consistently across all document contexts, including:

* Regular paragraphs
* List items
* Definition entries
* Section introductions

=== Paragraph Organization

Paragraphs in the
{mcm_document}
follow these structural principles:

* {mcm_blank_line_s}
appear only between true paragraphs
* No
{mcm_blank_line_s}
between sentences within a paragraph
* No
{mcm_blank_line_s}
around source code blocks or list items
* Each paragraph focuses on a single concept or relationship

Example paragraph structure:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The system initializes its core components.
Each component performs self-diagnostics.
The
{excm_validator}
confirms system health.

The initialization process then moves to external systems.
The
{excm_connector}
establishes required links.
Each connection undergoes verification.
----

=== Special Cases

==== List Items

The
{mcm_document}
presents list items
with proper term integration
and conceptual breaks:

[source,asciidoc]
----
* The first item spans
{excm_component}
boundaries
* A second item demonstrates
{excm_process}
flow
* The final item shows
{excm_result}
formatting
----

==== Definition Entries

Definition entries follow standard sentence breaks while maintaining definition list structure:

The single space following `::` is necessary.

[source,asciidoc]
----
[[excm_term]]
{excm_example}:: 
The primary definition establishes core meaning.
Additional sentences expand understanding.
The
{excm_linked_term}
shows relationship handling.
----

==== Source Code Boundaries

Source code blocks establish strict boundaries for
{mcm_curation_action_s}.
These boundaries protect code content while maintaining proper term integration in surrounding text:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The
{excm_processor}
implements this interface:

[source,java]
-----
public interface Processor {
    void process();
    Status check();
}
-----

The implementation ensures
{excm_compliance}
with system requirements.
----

{mcm_curation_action_s}
respect these boundaries by:

* Maintaining term integration and line breaks in surrounding text
* Treating code block contents as opaque
* Preserving code formatting without modification
* Excluding code content from term matching and promotion analysis

This boundary pattern ensures code examples remain stable through document evolution while maintaining proper conceptual integration in their context.

=== Punctuation Rules

Punctuation follows standard English conventions regardless of line breaks:

* Sentence-ending punctuation stays with the sentence
* Commas remain with their preceding phrase
* Quotation marks enclose their content without breaks
* Parentheses maintain content unity

Example punctuation handling:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The system (version 2.0) activates its components.
The
{excm_processor}
begins its "primary sequence."
After initialization,
the system reports "ready."
----

=== Document Mechanics

=== {mcm_mapping_section} Constraints

The
{mcm_mapping_section}
enforces alignment rules to enhance readability and change tracking:

* The `<<` of each
{mcm_domain}
aligns to columns that are multiples of 10
* When entries require wider alignment, all lines adjust to the next larger multiple
* {mcm_attribute_reference_s}
maintain consistent indentation

[source,asciidoc]
----
// tag::mapping-example[]
:excm_doc_short:              <<excm_short,Short Term>>
:excm_doc_longer_term:        <<excm_longer,Longer Term>>
:excm_doc_much_longer_term:   <<excm_much_longer,Much Longer Term>>
// end::mapping-example[]
----

{mcm_normalization}
maintains these alignments during curation unless expansion is required.

=== Document Source Management

The
{mcm_document}
maintains compatibility with line-based tools
while preserving semantic structure.

This means:

* Favoring AsciiDoc's native list markers
over hardcoded sequence numbers

* Preserving processor directives
that enable clean rendering

* Ensuring changes remain visible
in both source differencing tools
and rendered comparisons

The goal is maintaining a document that:

* Shows ancestry clearly in source
* Renders cleanly through AsciiDoc
* Displays changes meaningfully in diffs
* Supports post-rendering analysis

=== Term Hierarchy Management

When
{mcm_linked_term_s}
appear to form hierarchical relationships, the
{mcm_document}
flattens this hierarchy through specific patterns:

* Each
{mcm_linked_term_s}
receives first-class presentation in the
{mcm_mapping_section}
* Related
{mcm_linked_term_s}
maintain separate definitions
* Relationships express through careful definition crafting

Example of hierarchical
{mcm_linked_term_s}
handling:

[source,asciidoc]
----
:excm_doc_validation:             <<excm_validation,Validation>>
:excm_doc_validation_rule:        <<excm_validation_rule,Validation Rule>>
:excm_doc_validation_set:         <<excm_validation_set,Validation Set>>
----

The definitions then express relationships:

[source,asciidoc]
----
[[excm_validation]]
{excm_doc_validation}:: 
The process of applying rules to ensure content correctness.

[[excm_validation_rule]]
{excm_doc_validation_rule}:: 
A specific constraint within the {excm_doc_validation} framework.

[[excm_validation_set]]
{excm_doc_validation_set}:: 
A collection of {excm_doc_validation_rule_s} that work together.
----

== Definition Patterns

=== Definition Form Patterns

Each
{mcm_linked_term}
in a
{mcm_document}
requires an anchor target and a definition.
The form determines how these elements are structured.

[[mcm_annotation]]
{mcm_annotation}::
A comment line providing type categorization or metadata about terms in a
{mcm_document}.
{mcm_annotation_s}
are ignored during first-order document reading.
When a referencing system is in context,
{mcm_annotation_s}
provide structured metadata interpreted by that system.
The content contains space-separated
{mcm_linked_term_s}
from the referencing system, which is typically out-of-context during first-order reading.
The grammar, interpretation, and placement rules for
{mcm_annotation_s}
are defined by the referencing system, not MCM.
+
{mcm_annotation_s}
use prefix-discriminated form: `//ax...` — no space after `//`, where the initial characters match a registered prefix identifying the referencing system.
The full content after `//` is interpreted by the referencing system as space-separated prefix terms.
+
The mechanical discriminator is the character immediately following `//`.
A space character indicates a regular comment.
A letter with no intervening space indicates an
{mcm_annotation}.
+
Registered prefixes:
+
* `ax` — Axial Lexicon

[[mcm_form_deflist]]
{mcm_form_deflist}::
The standard pattern using AsciiDoc definition list syntax.
The anchor target appears on its own line, followed by an optional
{mcm_annotation}
then the term reference with `::` and definition text.
Use for standalone terms with self-contained meaning.

[source,asciidoc]
----
[[term_anchor]]
//axl_voices axk_motif
{term_reference}::
Definition text following sentence-per-line rules.
----

[[mcm_form_bullet]]
{mcm_form_bullet}::
A pattern for terms that belong to a parent structure or sequence.
Each term appears as a bullet item under its parent section.
The anchor target appears after the bullet marker, followed by an optional
{mcm_annotation}
then the term reference with definition prose.
Use for fields of a structure or steps in a procedure.

[source,asciidoc]
----
* [[field_anchor]]
//axl_voices axr_member
{field_reference} defines the specific meaning within the parent structure.
----

[[mcm_form_section]]
{mcm_form_section}::
A pattern where the section heading itself is the term.
The anchor target appears before the section marker, followed by an optional
{mcm_annotation}
then the section heading containing the term reference.
The section content constitutes the definition.
Use for terms whose meaning requires extended explanation or contains nested child terms.

[source,asciidoc]
----
[[sequence_anchor]]
//axl_voices axo_sequence
=== {sequence_reference}

The sequence executes these steps:

* [[step_one_anchor]]
//axl_voices axo_step
First step description.
----

=== Definition Organization Patterns

The
{mcm_document}
supports three primary patterns for organizing
{mcm_definition_section_s}:

==== Traditional Grouped Definitions

[source,asciidoc]
----
== Core Terms

[[excm_term1]]
{excm_term_one}:: 
Primary definition establishing core meaning.
Subsequent sentences expand understanding, though this is at most one paragraph.

[[excm_term2]]
{excm_term_two}:: 
Related term definition showing relationships.
----

==== Distributed Definitions

Definitions may accompany relevant content sections:

[source,asciidoc]
----
== System Components

Here we explore key architectural elements.

[[excm_component]]
{excm_sys_component}:: 
Definition tied directly to its context.

The discussion continues...
----

==== Domain-Specific Glossaries

[source,asciidoc]
----
== Technical Terms

[[excm_tech1]]
{excm_tech_term_one}:: 
Technical definition with domain focus.

[[excm_tech2]]
{excm_tech_term_two}:: 
Related technical concept definition.
----

=== {mcm_category}, {mcm_variant}, and {mcm_domain} Usage

The
{mcm_category},
{mcm_variant},
and
{mcm_domain}
work together to maintain precise term relationships and variations.

==== {mcm_category} Rules

* Each
{mcm_category}
declares its scope in the
{mcm_mapping_section}
header
* {mcm_category_ies}
distinguish terms by conceptual role
* New
{mcm_category_ies}
require explicit declaration

Example
{mcm_category}
declarations:

[source,asciidoc]
----
// exn_: Noun concepts - entities and objects
// exv_: Verb concepts - actions and operations
----

==== Variant Patterns

Common
{mcm_variant_s}
follow consistent patterns:

[source,asciidoc]
----
:exn_word:                   <<excm_word,Word>>
:exn_word_s:                 <<excm_word,Words>>
:exn_word_p:                 <<excm_word,Word's>>
:exv_act:                    <<excm_act,Act>>
:exv_act_s:                  <<excm_act,Acts>>
:exv_act_ed:                 <<excm_act,Acted>>
:exv_act_ing:                <<excm_act,Acting>>
----

{mcm_normalization}
enforces consistent application of these patterns across the document.

==== {mcm_domain} Pattern

Each term definition establishes a unique anchor using snake case:

[source,asciidoc]
----
// Domain declaration: excm_<canonical_term>
----

== Task Lens Support

{mcm_concept_model_s}
support focused work contexts through
{mcm_task_lens_es}
that emphasize relevant concepts while suppressing distractions.
See the
{mcm_task_lens_guide}
for compilation specifications and usage patterns.

=== Task Lens Structure

Each
{mcm_task_lens}
follows this pattern:

[source,asciidoc]
----
=== [Task Lens Name]

**Purpose**: [One sentence describing the work this lens enables]

**Gestalt**: [Metaphorical description guiding term interpretation]

**Core Activation**:

* {linked_term} - [Aspect emphasis if needed]
* {linked_term} - [Aspect emphasis if needed]

**Expanded Activation**:

* {linked_term} - [Reduced detail focus]
* {linked_term} - [Reduced detail focus]

**Suppressed Activation**:

* List of {linked_term_s} to remain dormant
* Additional suppression patterns

**Critical Relationships**:

* {term_a} ↔ {term_b}: [What aspect matters]
* {term_c} → {term_d}: [Specific mechanism focus]
----

Multiple
{mcm_task_lens_es}
may share terms but apply different
{mcm_focal_depth_s}
and aspect emphasis based on their purpose.

=== Blank Line Exception for Task Lenses

Despite the general document rule against
{mcm_blank_line_s}
around list items,
{mcm_task_lens}
sections require specific blank lines for valid asciidoc parsing:

* One blank line after each `**Section**:` header before its list
* One blank line between major sections
* No blank lines between list items within a section

This exception exists because asciidoc requires blank lines before bulleted lists to parse correctly, and the structured nature of
{mcm_task_lens_es}
makes the blank line placement predictable and consistent.

== Actor Definitions

[[mcm_editor]]
{mcm_editor}::
The human originator of
{mcm_concept_model_s}
and constraints.
The
{mcm_editor}
guides document evolution, establishes patterns, and directs transformations while maintaining conceptual integrity.

[[mcm_digital_mind]]
{mcm_digital_mind}::
An agent, typically an LLM, transforming
{mcm_document_s}
under
{mcm_editor}
direction and document constraints.
The
{mcm_digital_mind}
performs well-defined
{mcm_curation_action_s}
while preserving document patterns and relationships.

== {mcm_curation_action_s}

[[mcm_curation_action]]
{mcm_curation_action}::
A process executed by a
{mcm_digital_mind}
to adjust a
{mcm_document}
as directed by the
{mcm_editor}.
Each
{mcm_curation_action}
has a specific focus and constraints, modifying only those aspects of the
{mcm_document}
that fall within its defined scope.

[[mcm_normalization]]
{mcm_normalization}::
A
{mcm_curation_action}
that brings a
{mcm_document}
to canonical form through two phases.

*Phase 1: Text Normalization*

Applies consistent formatting throughout the
{mcm_document}
body:

* One sentence per line
* Each
{mcm_linked_term}
on its own line
* Commas and punctuation attached to preceding element
* {mcm_blank_line_s}
only between paragraphs and definition entries
* Preserve source code block contents without modification

*Phase 2: Mapping Section Normalization*

Maintains strict structural integrity of the
{mcm_mapping_section}:

* Preserve
{mcm_category}
comment headers as
{mcm_category_group}
delimiters
* Within each
{mcm_category_group},
align `<<` to the smallest multiple of 10 columns that accommodates the longest
{mcm_attribute_reference}
* Maintain alphabetical ordering by display text within each
{mcm_category_group}
* One
{mcm_attribute_reference}
per line

[[mcm_category_group]]
{mcm_category_group}::
A contiguous block of
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
in the
{mcm_mapping_section}
preceded by a category comment header.
Each
{mcm_category_group}
maintains independent column alignment,
allowing terms to be added without affecting other groups.

[[mcm_demotion_analysis]]
{mcm_demotion_analysis}::
A
{mcm_curation_action}
that identifies
{mcm_linked_term_s}
whose formal status may no longer justify their maintenance overhead.
It suggests candidates for demotion to
{mcm_fundamental_text}
based on criteria such as:
* Rare usage outside their own definition
* Lack of meaningful variation requiring
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
* Minimal role in expressing relationships between other terms
* Terms whose precise meaning has become self-evident in context

[[mcm_link_validation]]
{mcm_link_validation}::
A
{mcm_curation_action}
that verifies the mechanical integrity of the
{mcm_document_p}
linking system without modification:

* Reports
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
that lack corresponding anchors
* Reports anchors that lack corresponding
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
* Validates
{mcm_domain}
format compliance
* Identifies malformed link syntax

This action produces a diagnostic report for
{mcm_editor}
review.
Repairs are performed manually or through targeted
{mcm_curation_action_s}.

[[mcm_promotion_analysis]]
{mcm_promotion_analysis}:: 
A
{mcm_curation_action}
that identifies frequently occurring or conceptually significant elements in
{mcm_fundamental_text}
that warrant promotion to
{mcm_linked_term}
status.
It suggests candidates based on:
* Frequency in definitions of other
{mcm_linked_term_s}
* Use in expressing core conceptual relationships
* Occurrence in multiple distinct contexts
* Words whose precise meaning needs maintenance across document evolution

[[mcm_term_matching]]
{mcm_term_matching}::
A
{mcm_curation_action}
that identifies instances where concepts corresponding to existing
{mcm_linked_term_s}
appear in text without proper linking:
* Identifies important terms that warrant linking
* Proposes what should be linked

[[mcm_validation_summary]]
{mcm_validation_summary}::
An advisory report produced by
{mcm_link_validation}
that documents the state of the
{mcm_document}
without making modifications.
The
{mcm_validation_summary}
enables the
{mcm_editor}
to identify issues requiring attention.

== Word Selection

Prefix discipline governs the left half of every persistent name;
this section governs the right half — the word itself.
The rules below bind the word part of every
{mcm_quoin},
the display text of every
{mcm_linked_term},
and any persistent name a system mints: verbs, nouns, file words, command words.
They constrain births, not the living —
existing names are swept only by deliberate remint tooling, never piecemeal.

* *Semantic uniqueness.*
One meaning per word across all persistent names, repo-wide.
A word already bound to a meaning anywhere in the system is unavailable for a second meaning, however distant the domain.
* *Vocabulary isolation.*
Words mint from source domains alien to software, cloud infrastructure, CI/CD, and corporate project management.
A word with heavy ambient presence in software prose is a
{mcm_trodden_word}
and is disqualified regardless of semantic fit.
Domain choice makes collision structurally impossible;
word-level checks merely verify.
* *The grep gate.*
A candidate word is verified by repo-wide grep before adoption;
a clean landing is a minting precondition, not a nicety.
* *Rare but real.*
Prefer rare, real words whose true meaning does semantic work.
Concrete, physical, sensory images lodge across sessions in ways abstract labels cannot.
Abbreviations spend recognition to save characters — the wrong trade.
* *Mint into an* {mcm_asterism}.
A new word joins a coherent metaphor family wherever one fits;
a lone orphan word is weaker than a family member.
* *Audible register.*
Sibling subsystems ride
{mcm_asterism_s}
whose registers are distinguishable by ear,
so a stray term's home system is audible without lookup.

[[mcm_asterism]]
{mcm_asterism}::
A named metaphor family whose source-domain relationships mirror the system relationships it names.
The name is the gestalt: a recognizable figure of stars that is not itself a constellation and may span several —
as a vocabulary family may span
{mcm_concept_model_s}.
Each term inherits relational meaning from the family
(the coronet sits below the firemark as the hoof marking sits below the brand),
so the metaphor performs structural work beyond retrieval,
and a new term in an established family arrives nearly pre-learned —
for the
{mcm_digital_mind}
and the
{mcm_editor}
alike.
Founding specimen: Job Jockey's equestrian vocabulary (JJS0, Equestrian Vocabulary), with the racing and dressage layers as sibling families sharing one register.
Model-side rationale: `Memos/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`;
prefix-side sibling: `Memos/memo-20260110-acronym-selection-study.md`.

== Antipatterns

[[mcm_antipattern]]
{mcm_antipattern}::
A named, recurring document defect formally catalogued so that
{mcm_curation_action_s}
and reviews can cite it precisely.
Naming the defect is what makes it detectable and dischargeable;
an unnamed defect recurs because no rule can forbid it.

[[mcm_tidemark]]
{mcm_tidemark}::
An
{mcm_antipattern}:
version commentary embedded in a durable document — revision markers, dated change narration, "new in vX" annotations.
The name is the gestalt: the stain showing where the water once stood, saying nothing about where it is.
History-time knowledge belongs to the commit;
git log and blame carry every line's date and reason with a forcing function, while in-document
{mcm_tidemark_s}
drift and pollute present-tense reading.
A durable document — spec, guide, code, context file — speaks of itself only in the present tense.
A dated artifact — memo, scar, commit message — is itself a historical record and may carry revision metadata without fault.
Detection and drain moves are catalogued in ACG (ACGm_107).

[[mcm_trodden_word]]
{mcm_trodden_word}::
An
{mcm_antipattern}:
a persistent name minted from vocabulary worn featureless by ambient software prose —
open, close, run, lock, task, manager, handler, grant.
The name is the gestalt: a path so heavily walked that no single footprint is findable on it;
grep returns a thousand strangers.
A
{mcm_trodden_word}
fails retrieval for the
{mcm_digital_mind}
and search for the
{mcm_editor},
and invites the plan-versus-product bleed that vocabulary isolation exists to prevent.
Semantic fit never rescues it:
the word is disqualified before fit is weighed.

== Document Elements

[[mcm_fundamental_text]]
{mcm_fundamental_text}:: 
The basic sentences and phrases that comprise document text.
{mcm_fundamental_text}
follows specific formatting patterns in source while maintaining natural flow in rendered output.

[[mcm_notional_snippet]]
{mcm_notional_snippet}:: 
An exemplar that guides
{mcm_curation_action_s}.
While it may contain asciidoc noncompliance, the
{mcm_digital_mind}
uses it to understand patterns and generate compliant asciidoc output.

[[mcm_line_break]]
{mcm_line_break}::
A deliberate separation in a
{mcm_document}
that creates clear boundaries while preserving fluid rendering.
The
{mcm_line_break}
appears between independent sentences, before and after
{mcm_linked_term_s},
and between fragments surrounding
{mcm_linked_term_s}.
This pattern enables clean ancestry tracking while maintaining natural document flow.

[[mcm_blank_line]]
{mcm_blank_line}::
A purposeful demarcation in the
{mcm_document}
that separates major structural elements such as definition entries, sections, and lists.
The
{mcm_blank_line}
appears only between these elements, never within them.

== Term Management Elements

[[mcm_attribute_reference]]
{mcm_attribute_reference}::
A variable-style reference that connects a
{mcm_category}
prefixed attribute name to a specific
{mcm_domain}
and replacement text potentially with multiple
{mcm_variant_s}.
The
{mcm_attribute_reference}
forms the mechanical foundation for
{mcm_linked_term}
implementation in the
{mcm_document}.
Each
{mcm_attribute_reference}
follows strict formatting rules defined in the
{mcm_mapping_section}.

[[mcm_quoin]]
{mcm_quoin}::
A formal identifier with full cataloguing in the
{mcm_concept_model}:
{mcm_attribute_reference}
in the
{mcm_mapping_section},
anchor at the definition site, and definition text.
The cornerstone that locks the structure — addressable from anywhere, tracked across the constellation.
A
{mcm_quoin}
is the Diptych-era term for what
{mcm_linked_term}
describes in MCM's AsciiDoc expression.

[[mcm_inlay]]
{mcm_inlay}::
A formal identifier following the prefix naming discipline that exists only within a subdocument's scope.
No
{mcm_attribute_reference},
no anchor, no
{mcm_mapping_section}
entry.
An
{mcm_inlay}
is set permanently into a surface — recognized by its prefix pattern — but not catalogued in the parent document's lexicon.
Used by hierarchy markers that need to formally identify elements without elevating them to
{mcm_quoin}
status.

[[mcm_sprue]]
{mcm_sprue}::
A literal wire-level token in backtick delimiters representing a serialized name.
No
{mcm_attribute_reference},
no anchor, no
{mcm_mapping_section}
entry, no prefix naming discipline required.
A
{mcm_sprue}
is the exact character sequence as it appears in a wire format — a JSON property key, an API field name, a protocol identifier.
May originate from external schemas or foreign conventions.
A
{mcm_sprue}
that follows the prefix naming discipline is also an
{mcm_inlay};
one that does not is recognized solely by its backtick delimiters.
Used by arity rules when annotations read literal wire names from following text.

[[mcm_category]]
{mcm_category}::
A prefix mechanism that classifies
{mcm_linked_term_s}
by their conceptual role.
Each
{mcm_category}
must be declared in the
{mcm_mapping_section}
header and follows consistent patterns for classifying terms by type (e.g., 'doc_' for document elements, 'term_' for terminology elements).

[[mcm_variant]]
{mcm_variant}::
A systematic modification of a base term that maintains its core meaning while expressing a different form.
Common
{mcm_variant_s}
include plurals (_s), possessives (_p), and verb conjugations (_ed, _ing), each following patterns defined in the
{mcm_mapping_section}.

[[mcm_domain]]
{mcm_domain}::
A unique identifier that anchors a term's definition within the document structure.
Each
{mcm_domain}
uses snake case formatting and maintains consistent patterns (e.g., 'mcm_<canonical_term>') to enable reliable linking and reference.

[[mcm_task_lens]]
{mcm_task_lens}::
A focused activation pattern within a
{mcm_concept_model}
that emphasizes specific terms and relationships for particular work contexts.
Each
{mcm_task_lens}
defines which
{mcm_linked_term_s}
activate at what
{mcm_focal_depth}
and guides interpretation through gestalt descriptions and critical relationship specifications.


[[mcm_task_lens_guide]]
{mcm_task_lens_guide}::
A separate guide document that specifies how to compile task-focused representations from a full
{mcm_concept_model}.
The
{mcm_task_lens_guide}
defines lens structure patterns, compilation mechanics, and
{mcm_focal_depth}
activation rules for creating work-optimized views of concept constellations.

[[mcm_lens_specification]]
{mcm_lens_specification}::
The structured definition within a
{mcm_task_lens}
that categorizes
{mcm_linked_term_s}
by their
{mcm_focal_depth}
and provides aspect focusing guidance.
The
{mcm_lens_specification}
enables mechanical compilation of task-optimized representations from the full
{mcm_concept_model}.

[[mcm_focal_depth]]
{mcm_focal_depth}::
The activation level of a
{mcm_linked_term}
within a
{mcm_task_lens},
determining how much detail and relationship information to preserve.
The three
{mcm_focal_depth_s}
are Core (full detail with relationships), Expanded (supporting context), and Suppressed (explicitly dormant).
